final:
- **Agent Loop & Tool Registry**: Provides a model-agnostic harness with an agent loop that sends context, receives model actions, executes built-in tools (`list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `run_shell`), and feeds observations back.
- **Provider Adapters**: Supports multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, Groq, Azure OpenAI, and a local echo test provider, all via a subprocess protocol.
- **Run Transcripts & Evaluation**: Each task writes a JSON transcript under `runs/`; the evaluation runner measures success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, failure categories, and generates Markdown reports.
- **Trace & Replay Tools**: Saved runs can be rendered as Markdown flow traces (including Mermaid flowcharts) or replayed without calling a model for debugging.
- **Determinism Checks**: Supports running the same task repeatedly to measure consistency in success, final answer, and tool sequences, with reports saved under `evals/determinism/`.

- **LLM Agent Harness**: A lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic harness for LLM agents that includes an agent loop, tool registry, and provider adapters.
- **Features**: Run transcripts, evaluation reports, trace and replay tools, and determinism checks for consistency measurement.
- **Provider Support**: Connects to OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more via subprocess adapters.
- **Evaluation**: Includes a benchmark runner that checks final status, tool calls, text expectations, latency, token usage, cost, and failure categories.
- **Tools**: Built-in `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, and `run_shell` (disabled by default); shell access requires `--allow-shell` flag.

- LLM Agent Harness is a lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic harness for LLM agents, providing an agent loop, tool registry (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), and provider adapters for many LLM APIs.
- It includes run transcripts, evaluation reports (success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, failure categories), trace visualization, replay tools, and determinism checks.
- Quick start: run `python3 -m agent_harness.cli "task" --llm mock` from the project folder.
- Providers are subprocess adapters; environment variables (e.g., DEEPSEEK_API_KEY) are used for API keys; the project supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, Groq, and more.
- The evaluation benchmark under evals/ covers various tasks, and failure classification includes categories like planning_error, tool_error, timeout, hallucination, permission_denied, etc.

- **LLM Agent Harness**: A lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic harness for LLM agents.
- **Core Features**: Includes an agent loop, tool registry (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), provider adapters for multiple LLM APIs, run transcripts, evaluation reports, trace/replay tools, and determinism checks.
- **Provider Support**: Connects to OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more via subprocess adapters.
- **Evaluation & Benchmarking**: Built-in evaluation runner with tasks, failure classification, cost estimation, and determinism consistency measurements.
- **Shell & Security**: Shell tool disabled by default; extensible with permission policies, Web UI/TUI, and concurrent execution plans.

- **Lightweight & Model-Agnostic**: A locally runnable harness for LLM agents, with an agent loop, tool registry (`list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `run_shell`), and provider adapters for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more.
- **Subprocess Protocol**: Providers communicate via JSON over stdin/stdout; the harness sends messages/tools and receives structured responses with thought, tool calls, or a final answer.
- **Evaluation & Reporting**: Built-in benchmark runner evaluates tasks against expected outcomes (status, tool calls, text), writes JSONL results, Markdown reports, and transcripts; failure categories classify errors (e.g., planning, tool, timeout, hallucination).
- **Trace & Replay**: Every run generates a JSON transcript that can be rendered as a Markdown trace (including Mermaid flowcharts) or replayed for debugging without calling a model.
- **Determinism & Safety**: Runs the same task repeatedly to measure consistency; shell access is disabled by default and must be explicitly enabled with `--allow-shell`; future extensions include permission policies and a Web/TUI.

- **Agent Harness:** A lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic harness for LLM agents, providing an agent loop, built-in tools (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), and provider adapters for multiple LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, etc.).
- **Run Transcripts & Evaluation:** Each run saves a JSON transcript under `runs/`. Evaluation reports include success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, failure categories, and cost breakdown.
- **Provider Architecture:** Providers are subprocess adapters that communicate via JSON over stdin/stdout. The harness sends a JSON payload with messages, tools, and max_tool_calls; the provider returns a JSON with thought, tool_calls, and final.
- **Evaluation Benchmark:** A built-in benchmark under `evals/` with tasks covering directory listing, file operations, shell access control, error handling, and more. It generates JSONL results, Markdown reports, and per-run transcripts.
- **Trace, Replay & Determinism:** Trace visualization renders runs as Markdown with Mermaid flowcharts. Replay replays saved runs without calling a model. Determinism runs the same task repeatedly to measure consistency (success, final answer, tool sequence).

- Lightweight, model-agnostic harness for LLM agents that runs locally, providing an agent loop, tool registry, and provider adapters.
- Supports multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, etc.) via a subprocess protocol, with built-in tools like list_dir, read_file, write_file, and run_shell.
- Includes evaluation benchmark with failure classification, run transcripts, trace visualization, and replay capabilities.
- Offers determinism checks to measure consistency across repeated runs, with reports on success and final answer consistency.
- Designed for extensibility with future plans for permission policies, Web UI/TUI, and concurrent task execution.

- **Lightweight Agent Harness**: A locally runnable, model-agnostic system for LLM agents with an agent loop, tool registry (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), and provider adapters for multiple LLM APIs.
- **Evaluation & Reporting**: Built-in evaluation runner that checks success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, and failure categories, outputting JSONL results and Markdown reports.
- **Trace & Replay**: Every run produces a JSON transcript; tools to render traces as Markdown flowcharts (with Mermaid) and replay recorded executions without calling the model.
- **Determinism Checks**: Run the same task multiple times to measure consistency in success, final answer, and tool sequence, with reports on stability.
- **Provider Ecosystem**: Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more via subprocess adapters, with environment variable configuration and optional SDK dependencies.

- **Lightweight Agent Harness**: A model-agnostic, locally runnable harness that manages agent loops, tool execution, and observation feedback.
- **Built-in Tools & Provider Adapters**: Includes tools like list_dir, read_file, write_file, and run_shell. Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.) via subprocess adapters.
- **Evaluation & Reporting**: Provides an evaluation runner with detailed metrics (success rate, latency, token usage, cost, failure categories). Results are saved as JSONL and Markdown reports.
- **Trace, Replay & Determinism**: Offers trace visualization (Markdown flow with Mermaid charts), replay of saved runs, and tools to measure consistency across repeated task executions.
- **Extensible & Protocol-Driven**: Uses a simple JSON subprocess protocol for provider communication. Includes example setup instructions, environment variable management, and planned enhancements (permission policies, Web UI, concurrent execution).

1. **Lightweight, model-agnostic agent harness** – Locally runnable, supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.).
2. **Built-in tool registry** – Includes core tools: `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, and `run_shell` (disabled by default).
3. **Evaluation and tracking** – Generates run transcripts, evaluation reports, and supports deterministic consistency checks.
4. **Trace and replay** – Saves run traces as Markdown with Mermaid flowcharts; replays without calling a model.
5. **Extensible provider protocol** – Uses subprocess-based JSON communication; custom providers can be added easily.

- **Lightweight LLM Agent Harness**: A locally runnable, model-agnostic harness that provides an agent loop, tool registry, and provider adapters for multiple LLM APIs.
- **Built-in Tools and Providers**: Includes tools like `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, and `run_shell`, plus adapters for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more.
- **Evaluation and Benchmarking**: Comes with a evaluation runner that checks success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, and failure categories; results are saved as JSONL and Markdown reports.
- **Trace and Replay**: Every run generates a JSON transcript that can be rendered as a Markdown trace with a Mermaid flowchart, or replayed without calling a model for debugging.
- **Determinism Testing**: Supports repeated runs of the same task to measure consistency, generating reports on answer stability and tool sequence reproducibility.

- **Lightweight, model-agnostic agent harness**: Provides an agent loop, tool registry (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), and multiple provider adapters for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more.
- **Run transcripts and evaluation**: Each task writes a JSON transcript under `runs/`, and the eval runner generates reports on success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, failure categories, and consistency.
- **Trace and replay**: Saved runs can be rendered as Markdown flow traces with Mermaid diagrams or replayed for debugging without calling a model.
- **Determinism checks**: The same task can be run repeatedly to measure stability, with consistency reports covering success, final answer, and tool sequence.
- **Subprocess protocol**: Providers communicate via JSON over stdin/stdout, and the harness supports provider-specific setup via environment variables (e.g., `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY`).

- **LLM Agent Harness**: A lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic framework for building and testing LLM agents.
- **Core Features**: Includes an agent loop, built-in tools (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), and support for multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.).
- **Evaluation & Reporting**: Provides evaluation benchmarks with success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost estimation, and failure classification (e.g., planning_error, hallucination).
- **Trace & Replay**: Every run generates a JSON transcript that can be rendered as a Markdown trace or replayed for debugging and analysis.
- **Determinism Testing**: Supports running the same task multiple times to measure consistency in success, final answers, and tool sequences.

- **Lightweight, model-agnostic harness**: A locally runnable system for LLM agents, supporting multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, etc.) via subprocess adapters.
- **Core components**: Agent loop, built-in tools (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), provider adapters, run transcripts, evaluation reports, trace/replay, and determinism checks.
- **Subprocess protocol**: Harness sends JSON context; provider returns JSON with thought, tool calls, or final answer. Tools are executed and observations fed back.
- **Evaluation and benchmarking**: Includes an eval runner for tasks like file operations, summarization, and shell denial, with failure classification, cost tracking, and Markdown reports.
- **Optional shell access and extensions**: Shell tool is disabled by default but can be enabled for trusted tasks. Planned extensions include permission policies, Web UI, and concurrent execution.

- **LLM Agent Harness**: A lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic framework for building and testing LLM agents, with built-in tools (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell) and support for multiple providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.).
- **Core Features**: Includes an agent loop, tool registry, provider adapters, run transcripts, evaluation reports, trace visualization, replay, and determinism checks.
- **Provider Architecture**: Each provider runs as a subprocess adapter communicating via JSON over stdin/stdout, making it easy to add new providers.
- **Evaluation & Benchmarking**: Comes with a benchmarking system under `evals/` that checks expected status, tool calls, text in answers, latency, token usage, cost, and failure categories.
- **Shell Access & Extensions**: Shell execution is disabled by default for safety; planned extensions include permission policies, a Web UI/TUI, and concurrent task execution.

- **Lightweight LLM Agent Harness**: A locally runnable, model-agnostic framework for running LLM agents with an agent loop, tool registry, and provider adapters.
- **Built-in Tools and Providers**: Includes tools like `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, and `run_shell`, and supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.) via subprocess adapters.
- **Evaluation and Benchmarking**: Provides an evaluation runner that checks success rate, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, and failure categories, with results saved as JSONL and Markdown reports.
- **Trace and Replay**: Every run generates a JSON transcript that can be rendered as a Markdown trace (with Mermaid flowchart) or replayed for debugging without calling the model.
- **Determinism Testing**: Can run the same task multiple times to measure consistency in outputs, tool sequences, and latency, producing consistency reports.

- **Lightweight LLM Agent Harness**: A model-agnostic, locally runnable framework for building and testing LLM agents.
- **Tool Registry**: Includes built-in tools like `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, and `run_shell` (shell disabled by default).
- **Provider Adapters**: Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, DeepSeek, etc.) via a subprocess protocol.
- **Evaluation and Debugging**: Provides run transcripts, evaluation reports, trace visualization, replay, and determinism checks.
- **Extensible Architecture**: Designed for adding permission policies, Web UI/TUI, and concurrent task execution.

- **LLM Agent Harness**: A lightweight, locally runnable, model-agnostic harness for LLM agents, providing an agent loop, tool registry, and provider adapters.
- **Key Features**: Includes built-in tools (list_dir, read_file, write_file, run_shell), run transcripts, evaluation reports, trace/replay, and determinism checks.
- **Provider Support**: Connects to OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and more as subprocess adapters exchanging JSON.
- **Evaluation & Benchmarking**: Comes with an evaluation runner that checks success rates, tool accuracy, latency, token usage, cost, and failure categories.
- **Extras**: Offers trace visualization, run replay, deterministic consistency checks, and shell access control via command-line flags.

- **Agent Loop & Tool Registry**: Provides a lightweight, model-agnostic harness that runs an agent loop, sending context to an LLM, executing tools, and feeding observations back. Includes built-in tools like list_dir, read_file, write_file, and run_shell.
- **Provider Adapters**: Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, DeepSeek, etc.) via subprocess adapters, making it easy to switch between models without code changes.
- **Evaluation & Transcripts**: Offers a benchmark system with eval tasks that check final status, tool calls, text, latency, token usage, and cost. Every run generates a JSON transcript and optional Markdown traces.
- **Determinism & Replay**: Includes tools for running the same task multiple times to measure consistency, and replaying saved runs without calling a model for debugging.
- **Subprocess Protocol**: Communicates with providers via a simple JSON stdin/stdout protocol, returning tool calls or final answers as structured JSON.

- **LLM Agent Harness** is a lightweight, model-agnostic, locally runnable framework for building and testing LLM agents, featuring an agent loop, built-in tools, and provider adapters.
- It supports multiple LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Azure OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, and a local echo test provider, each configured via environment variables.
- The project includes evaluation, trace, replay, and determinism tools to assess agent performance, visualize execution flows, and measure consistency across runs.
- A subprocess protocol defines how the harness communicates with providers via JSON over stdin/stdout, enabling easy integration of custom or third-party models.
- Built-in tools include `list_dir`, `read_file`, `write_file`, and `run_shell` (disabled by default), with plans for stricter permission policies and a Web/TUI interface.
